review_text:
Awesome place Fingal Bay, great beach and takeaway close by.
Good for SUPER, mini mals, body boards and snorkelling.
Great park, very clean,  quiet. Strongly recommend
Grey Nomads back to rockhampton
This park is absolutely 5 star. Grounds, amenities, staff, dog friendly, beach directly opposite which is stunning. The most perfect family weekend away. Can't wait to get back.

Great location, nice holiday destination.
Good sized caravan sites. Communal areas and toilets could be kept cleaner.
Nice sized pool.
Great park across from beach with walking distance to shops and restaurant.
Great park and great location.
Visited family....great holiday spot. Well kept park with lots of facilities.
Excellent camping site in a beautiful location only 3 hours drive from Sydney.
Favourite beach/holiday spot in the world.

We had the pleasure of staying this January and have come back regularly for many years always enjoyed our time. Rooms are clean and the pool and grounds are perfect and staff are friendly. Great position close to a flagged beach as well.
Very nice family camp ground
Great park and staff, camp sites and facilities are always maintained and cleaned, plenty for the kids to do and wonderful beaches and sites to see.

No security at all on during holiday period. Kids riding e-bikes around at 40km/h with kids around and stealing scooters and bikes and to make it worse the staff were not interested in hearing about it. The toilets were clean and tidy but security is definitely needed to make the park safe for children
Awsome staff, awsome spot, awsome park

The best holiday park ever! we've been all the way up the east coast of Australia to FNQ. The staff here are amazing, nothing is a problem. So close to the beach and some cute shops in the main street

Seriously the best caravan park we have been to in 2 years of travel. Yes it’s a bit pricey especially for the ensuite drive thru sites but well worth it. Dog friendly and an awesome family atmosphere. Will we be back again-bloody oath. Well done Fingal Bay.
What an amazing place we have been loving coming here for 30 years. Cheers to many more 🌟🙌🌟

Highly recommended. Had a family get together in our vans. The motor home sites are to die for!
Clean amenities, close to the beach. Will be back
Peaceful and very close to the beach. Rooms clean and well maintained.
very good accommodation near the beach
Lovely caravan park,  neat and tidy amenities.  Cool pool and kids equipment.  Close to takeaways.  Tidy road for the kids to ride on.

What a great park!!!,we parked our van on a double slab (number 120)with a lot of space between us and our neighbours,lots of bushes and trees throughout the park.Amenities are very clean and helpful staff. The beach is great with plenty of coffee shops and restaurants nearby, will definitely be returning. Thanks again.
Staff were super helpful and sites were well played out. Great amenities and we also saw a Koala there! Beautiful beach near by!
Great park only thing missing are sun lounges around the pool, everything else first class.
The camp sites are amazing here, lovely leafy area, close to the pool and the beach. Will definitely be back

Three stars. Our van is a 6.9m inc drawbar and the rear annexe. We got a 5m slab in the center of the park. Had we have known it wasn't going to be a busy time we wouldve asked for a different spot but didnt want to be rude.
So to compensate we had the drawbar about 1ft from the road (it's suppose to be 1m from the road) so we could get as much slab as we could to fit our 2.5m rear annexe but was still 1.5m short of having that sadly.
Apart from that Fingal bay is an amazing spot, the park is very tidy and the showers are so clean, the best Ive seen. Lots of water pressure! The area is great for bicycles and trail walking. The permanent caravan/annexes homes are very tidy also. No clutter.

Amazing spacious ensuite sites, great location with an amazing beach in the bay.
Holiday park is top notch, definitely booking a return trip!

Amazing location right across the road from Fingal bay beach. We stayed on a powered caravan site that was large and well kept, with a concrete pad. Lots of trees and plants throughout the park providing ample shade. Amenenties appeared to be relitevly new and very neat and clean.
Stayed in a RV site, was very spacious, easy to park our 20ft van with ease, amenities are clean and taken care of, grounds are neat and tidy, happy to come back.

A beautifully maintained park with loads of amenities in a great location. We stayed in a cabin and were very impressed by the well kept gardens giving each cabin privacy.  Very clean and comfortable.

What a great clean Park with excellent facilities.
Close to shops and restaurants, and the Beach is just spectacular.
If you are looking for a great park on the beach, nothing will beat this.
You have to come here
Great park in a great location. Loving the lazy traveller option of the drive thru ensuite sites

We had an event booked in and was arranging accommodation for 10 families, to be told there was a 3 night min due to public holiday on the WEDNESDAY before, just money grabbing! You should be ashamed ! We'll never book another event in Fingal
Banging location, no hot water pressure. Couldn't have a shower in winter. Glad we weren't staying more than one night. Very pricey for basic amenities also.
Beautiful holiday park. Gets very busy on weekends. Very clean tidy park. Walk across the street is a beautiful beach.

We have a big van and tow vehicle so it's always a pleasure to find a caravan park with well laid out sites. The large sites at Fingal Bay Holiday Park were well separated so no tripping over your neighbours' cords and hoses. The site was easy to manoeuvre. The amenities are fairly new, in great condition and always clean. A very short walk to a nice park across the road which also provides beach access. A cafe, service station and small supermarket are just around the corner or it's an easy drive into the shops, restaurants and cafes of Nelson Bay.

We really enjoyed Fingal Bay caravan park from the friendly welcome to the spacious double concrete site. Amenities were excellent and the grounds well attended. Lovely safe beach to swim and walk. The surf club had an excellent cafe with efficient young staff
We’ve been here for two long weekends now and cannot recommend the location or facilities highly enough. The pool is bloody freezing all year round but the kids don’t seem to care!

The water line cut for half a day, big issue.
Also game room was nice, but it's almost all gambling machines for kids.
Love this place

The management have contempt for the guests in the camping area. This is due to a loud generator running 24/7 in the adjacent field to the camping area. This generator, running a mobile phone tower, emits well above the allowable limit for noise in the daytime and especially at nighttime. I went to reception to tell them and they fobbed me off. Most people wouldn’t go and find out what the noise was, there would just be a disturbing level of background noise. I went and investigated and found the tower with generator. Something needs to be done about this noise. It’s easy to just brush it aside and not take responsibility but that’s treating your guests with contempt.
Love this park. We had a motor home drive through, very spacious.  The amenities are fabulous,  very clean. The pool large, great cafes short walk.  ❤️

Travelling in our caravan. A great park and location but if we’d known none of the beaches were dog friendly we wouldn’t have come.
There is a good walking/cycling track though with glimpses of the sea! My husband took some beautiful photos which is as close as I got 🤣
There is a dog off leash area but that’s on the side of the road.

There is zero after hours service, none. We were locked out by the barrier system until a camper helped us out. This is no way to treat customers.  Also the WiFi was rubbish and when. Showers were good and clean. Won’t ever come back.

We were camping in a van on the Astro turf at front of park. Maintenance was very helpful in providing us with screw in pegs to secure our gazebo. Thanks to the camper that lent us a drill to get them out, as Maintenance did not return to get them out . Overall this is a great park , a bit noisy over the weekends but that's to be expected.  We were there 6 nights and managed 2 hot showers, the hot water system is a nightmare   so don't expect too much in that regard. We luckily had a friend staying at the resort down the road so we grabbed showers in the arvo down there. Reception is friendly and helpful. Laundry facilities are good. The pool is lovely and the location to the beach is spot on.
We would definitely stay again .
The cabin was perfect for a quick weekend getaway for a wedding, and location was perfect to everything you need. Service was excellent and the room was clean!

We have a permanent van here.
The amenities are ok.

Absolutely beautiful spot but the lack of hot water in the showers was a let down. The price to camp is quite expensive so you'd expect to have a hot shower at the end of the day.
Would rate 5 stars if you could have a hot shower.

The seclusion from Nelson Bay enables this site to be a destinational holiday location.
Amenities are clean and the ground staff are very obliging to assist.

Camp grounds are grassed covered with ample trees, it’s just a very organised site.

I’d recommend one bait freezer located in one area of the park to cater for the fishermen, just as your competitors provide. Think of the customer, instead of the staff whom may think it stinks as it doesn’t whilst the bait is frozen.

The pool is huge and can accommodate many people in, the beach is directly across the road and easily within walking distance.

Snack and ice creams are desired by most that I spoken to at the reception desk.

Stayed in a 2 bedroom villa
Was disappointed as 2nd bedroom only had 3 bunk style beds in it with very thin mattresses
Not what I wanted
Was also located other end of park from the wedding party I went with
Not happy at all
Come every year and never disappointed!!
הפתעה לטובה
Lovely place
Great holiday park, stayed at a powered tent site. Great amenities and very accommodating with our little dog. Will stay again!
Great park with lots of well maintained gardens

Very nice and clean holiday villas,
AC, pool, beach 2 min walk

Stayed in one of the 2 bedroom cabins which was presented in excellent condition. Reception staff very helpful as was the on line check in process. Great facilities including pool, BBQ and kitchen areas, together with areas for kids to play. Walk to beach, shops and nearby recreation club. Altogether a lovely experience. Would highly recommend to couples and families alike.
Great location with excellent facilities. The management team and staff have gone above and beyond in taking care of their guests. Highly recommended.

Giving Fingal Bay a five star rating due to their excellent customer service. We had our son get unexpectedly ill just prior to our holiday. They assisted us quickly with a last minute cancellation. We are very grateful for their thoughtful and quick service.
Great location at one of our coasts most special and spectacular beach/bay locations.

I stayed in a cabin. It was fantastic! Air con, verandah with chairs and table, great shower, comfy bed. Very comfortable and spotlessly clean. Also nice and quiet. The pool is fabulous and so warm and big enough for a proper swim. I'll be back for sure.

We came to the park about 4 weeks had such a great time that we wanted to come back again as soon as we could. First site was one of the new Astro turf sites at the front, they are good and give very easy access to the beach, second site was a tent site on the northern boundary and the shade of a big tree was excellent. Great park, very clean facilities and a top location. Love coming here and will be back again soon!
Clean and peaceful holiday park
Excellent park beach across the road
Very friendly staff and the place is clean
Quiet and close to the bay.
Lovely park. Nice facilities. Super close to the beach.

Originally we were going to stay at Ingenia soldiers point during September. We had to cancel due to family reasons. |I gave a months notice but they wouldn’t fully refund so I took credit. When I went to rebook, because it was the long weekend, the price went from $317 to nearly $800! (For a slab of concrete and a PowerPoint…)|Suffice to say, we canceled and they took $50 for our troubles. ||I am so glad we wasted $50 on that failed booking now. I’ve been to that Ingenia, but I’d never camped at Fingal bay. |Fingal met and exceeded my expectations! Excellent facilities, large park, friendly staff and beautiful pool with a great location next to the beach. AND all at a fraction of the price of the Ingenias in the area. |You should be proud of your beautiful park, but you should also pride yourself on the fact that whilst all the parks around you are more than DOUBLING their prices, you simply add a fair surcharge onto the price to cover public holiday wages, and leave it at that. |You should also be proud that whilst those same parks are imposing a 4 night minimum, forcing their patrons to extend their holiday and take a day of annual leave therefore defeating the purpose of the free day off a long weekend offers, you allow people to simply stay for the time we have been given. ||So thank you for being a great park and thank you for not bowing to the greedy tactics of your competitors. Well done!
Location is perfect

This Park is great. Clean n tidy good size space for our van and car. The only thing was a dog off lead causing havoc which was reported and hopefully dealt with after the second reported sighting to the office.
Absolutely beautiful spot

We stayed for one night in a camper back in March. |It was our first time visiting Australia and our first time using a campervan. |We were absolutely blown away with the facilities at Fingal bay. |The bathrooms were so clean and the showers were hot and well kept. |The location was amazing with the beach just a short 2 minutes across the road. |We wish we could've stayed and longer and will definitely return at some point.

A clean and well maintained holiday park that is dog friendly. Operated by council and which we found to be better to be maintained better than privately operated parks. Amenities were clean and the laundry you could pay via your credit card and not having to find coins to pay. Too cold to swim for us but the pool area was great and nearby was a playground for children. |Just around the corner within walking distance was a great fish and chip shop with the best potato cakes (scallops in NSW) I have eaten. Also in this little group of shops was an IGA, petrol station and a great little wine bar that was also dog friendly. While we were there there were 7 adults and 5 dogs.

We loved this park that is operated by council. Across the road from green parkland where a walking path has beautiful coastal vistas. The amenities were well maintained and clean. The laundry facilities enabled you to pay by card and not have to search for coins.
Excellent clean cabin,  Lots of space, comfortable bed.  Not much of a view, but it's a caravan park 🤷‍♂️.
Lovely park very well maintained and great location

Stopped in for a few days. Great park with well presented amenities cleaned regularly with grounds staff maintaining the premises daily. Staff were lovely and helpful.
Will be back soon

We had a drive through ensuite powered site. Ensuite was clean. Staff were friendly.
We found Fingal Bay was a little out of the way, but in no way was it a deterant,  just a short drive to Shoal Bay main drag.
Very tidy park.
Two of us stayed Fingal Bay Holiday Park whilst on a coastal road trip. Friendly staff, access to good ammenities, and well-maintained park. Thank you for our stay.

I have stayed at this caravan park 10+ times over the yrs. Always camping or on-site van. |I love this park but this latest experience was the worst we've had (we still loved it mind you)|Here are some things we picked up on- |- Dirty floor mats in cabins, hadn't been taken out and cleaned|- cobwebs and bugs all over cabins. You would atleast think cobwebs would be brushed down as apart of the cleaning. Or sprayed with insecticide every few months. I did see a red back spider in the Amenities block. |- all doors were Squeaky and didn't really close properly in the cabin. Made it a bit annoying with a sleeping baby. |- sliding entry door was very screechy/noisy/rough when we first got there. Reported it to maintenance who about 20hrs later come and replaced the Rollers which partly fixed the problem but was still rough. |- the TV was very small. Usually try not to watch much TV while on holidays but it was the first round of nrl so it was rude not to. |- no extraction fan in toilet||Now onto the positives|- the beach is just as beautiful as ever, right across the Rd|- the pool was heated and great for the kids |- the new(ish) Amenities block is great|- the laundry is excellent and accepts card ||We had a great trip and will be back once again. The best caravan park in Port stephens (don't tell anyone)

We had a couple of major issues with this holiday park.  First, when I booked in on arrival for an unpowered tent site, I requested and was charged for two night stay, the first night for myself only, the second night for an additional three adults.  Total was $158, which I thought excessive but , because of commitments to meet other hikers there, I paid up front.  The three additional guests (unknown to me) then paid for themselves (additional $60).  We then received an "overdue" noticed requiring us to pay a further amount to $250.  We refused, and they reluctantly scrapped the additional charges, but no refund or copy of the full receipt was offered.
A second issue was Saturday night, when a large group of Yobbos gathered in a nearby "permanent" van and partied with loud music till after 3am Sunday morning.  The security number was uncontactable, and we were reluctant to confront the partygoers as they were using a lot of obscene language. I would not recommend it to anyone.

Spent a week here and love it. The beach across the road is perfect for the kids, massive swimming pool, multiple clean bbq areas and the toilets very clean and maintained. Hope to be back in the future,lots of permanent sites, so book well ahead if your taking a van.
I stayed at tent site

Best campsite we’ve been to so far. My 5yo loved the pool. Only 10 mins drive to busier Port Stephens locations. We liked exploring during the day and then coming back to Fingal Bay in the afternoons to chill. Kitchen and Bathrooms were modern, clean, spacious and plenty available despite the campsite being busy.

Fingal bay was quiet and lovely. Local shops super friendly.
Such a good park. Fairly spacious, large pool and straight opposite the beach..

My brother and  my father and I own a onsite van here that we have had for a number of years and have very much enjoyed weekend and holliday stays at this park on a fairly regular basis for the purpose of relaxation and flexible accomodation at anytime . any people staying at this park soon realise just how lucky they are to be just across the road from the magnificent fingal bay beach and surrounding national park. I enjoy the facilities of this holliday park and the holliday atmosphere with just a few exceptions in regard to the lack of toilet and shower block maintenance from the park managment , as well as the appalling filthy mess left in the toilets by some of the many campers who visit this park.
Nice bay, good park, although a little crowded
Great place for the family for a holiday

We have been coming regularly in December for five years. The units are very clean, the staff is very helpful. 1 minutes walking to the sea. We had a very nice one week with friends this year too.
The only problem is that it would be great if they allocated a few units to animal-loving families.
Thanks
Just a long way from Orange NSW & you get old & no longer drive

In the cabins, they've changed it so that every second room has a queen sized bed.

For a family of 5, with two young men over 20, this is just rotten. My family and I have been going here for as long as I can remember, and they've suddenly needed to squeeze two young adults into a small cramped room with a bunk bed, a sliding door that doesn't close fully and only a dinky little rotating fan keeping us cool.

I didn't realise how uncomfortably my sister had been sleeping the last few years, but at least having just one person in the room benefited the less than ideal sleeping conditions.

Can't say I'm too impressed with the direction this place is going, nor do I think I'll bother going with my family next year.
Great facilities on site. Good pool with very good showers/toilets next to the pool. Nice eating/kitchen area. Across the road from the beach.

A lovely park, extremely well maintained amenities. Had 5 days stay. Will definitely be back. Office staff very efficient.
Fish & chips are a must at cafe nearby!
Fabulous cabin across the road from the beach
Had 2 family holidays at Fingal Bay. Adults and grandkids loved it

Great caravan Park. If you have a bigger van (21.6) like us, request a Drive Thru lot, RV lot or even an ensuite lot. The older sites are better suited to vans 18-19feet.
However, the facilities are excellent and very modern. The park is tidy with a mix of cabins, van sites and camping sites. Also very neat looking permanent vans. Close to a beautiful beach and cafes, 1 in each direction.
Dog and kid friendly. Activity room and pool is great for all kids, small and large alike.
We will be back. 👍
Location is amazing. The check-in experience is a disaster.
Great location, very good facilities and cabins

Our site was across from the amenities, kitchen and game room. All night long we heard a robotic voice saying "Stack 'em up!". The only door was a screen door which did nothing to block the sound. Yes I gave it a try some time after midnight.

Amenities were nice and clean. Kitchen was fine, no oven and bring your own pans and such, but good cooking space.

We paid $90 for a tent site. We don't need power, but there doesn't seem to be unpowered sites available at the campground.

Lovely park, with lots of shady trees and shrubs, located directly opposite beautiful Fingal Bay.
The shared facilities are spacious and well-maintained, and if you are travelling with young children, the family bathroom is a lifesaver. Bathrooms, laundry and camp kitchen are all modern and very logically set out
Tidy holiday park with very basic cabins. Hot water failed on day 2 and poor response from front desk staff. Location is a winner
Fabulous park great cabin wonderful amenities for campers.

What a great spot! We hired the 3 bedroom villa, it was beautiful and comfortable.
Park facilities are top notch, great for our family with 4 young kids.
A walk across from the beach and only a very short drive into shoal Bay or Nelson Bay if you want more action.
Will definitely be back!
Thanks for having us.
Beautiful part of the world

Amazing place 🌿🦜very clean rooms, all of the workers here are very friendly and happy to help you. There is alot of great amenities here as well for example,
🥎Tennis court's are right next door.
🕹️there is a games room with a kitchen.
🏊‍♂️ Awesome pools (open during warmer months of course.
👔There is a really nice laundry area.
🥏a kids play park with seating and BBQ
🏖️the beach is across the road

Very very clean cabins utensils etc
Very friendly
Wish could hire a caravan of the parks to have my dog stay but only allowed own caravan to have your dog stay understanding messes they could leave

Great Park Lovely n Clean
Wonderful  Staff
Great park although we were allocated a site where we needed a cut lunch and a guide to find the amenities. Price was a bit rich at $65 a night.
Nice caravan park opposite Final Bay beach. Great amenities and clean as.

I was travelling to the Gold Coast from Victoria in a caravan and this was my second stop for a couple of days, very friendly at reception the only issue I found was the sites were very tight and the one they put me on I didn't fit (My van is 7M) lucky for me the park was quite empty being the off season and I could pick another spot but I found the roads not wide enough and ended up on a corner site which was fine, I think when this place is busy getting a large van in and out might be a bit difficult.

The park amenities were excellent well maintained and serviced regularly, there is a games room for the kids a great camp kitchen and dining area, directly across the road from the park is a majestic beach I'm sure in summer this would be very busy and just a short stroll your in the small village shopping precinct with a quaint general store.

This park is situated about 50 minutes from the main highway so its a bit out of the way if your heading north but I think the area is well worth the detour.
Loved this park..cabins excellent..we didn't want to.leave
